  • Classical Combination Frequencies in a Morse potential

Classical Combination Bands: A Model System
  • Fortran API
  • View page source

Fortran API

Morse Dynamics

program  oscillator

Code to run the dynamics of a Morse oscillator

The dynamics parameters are read from input

The energy is plotted in energy_plot.dat for debugging

Position and momentum are plotted in phase_space.dat

Call to:

gradient(), potential()

subroutine  potential(omega, a, de, q, qdot, v)

Compute the Morse potential energy.

The potential is

\[V(q) = D_{e}(1 - \exp(aq))^2\]

where \(D_{e}\) is the dissociation energy and \(a\) controls the width/asymmetry of the Morse well.

Parameters:

  • omega [real] :: Harmonic frequency

  • a [real] :: Morse potential parameter

  • de [real] :: Morse potential parameter

  • q [real] :: Position

  • qdot [real] :: Position velocity

  • v [real] :: Potential

Called from:

oscillator

subroutine  gradient(omega, a, de, q, qdot, qddot)

Compute the acceleration for the Morse potential.

This returns the quantity used by the velocity-Verlet scheme.

Parameters:

  • omega [real] :: Harmonic frequency

  • a [real] :: Morse potential parameter

  • de [real] :: Morse potential parameter

  • q [real] :: Position

  • qdot [real] :: Position velocity

  • qddot [real] :: Force

Called from:

oscillator

Power Spectrum

program  powerspectrum1d

Compute 1D power spectrum

This program reads a time-dependent signal and computes its power spectrum using Fourier transform techniques.

The Fourier Transform parameters are in FT_input

Position and momentum are read in phase_space.dat

The position spectrum is printed in power_spectrum_q.dat

The momenta spectrum is printed in power_spectrum_p.dat
